message:
chore(fcp-e2e): fan out CapabilityVerifier::verify() owned-token signature to algolia/arxiv/asana compliance harnesses

Extends the earlier `fcp-e2e` migration (which updated 10 harnesses:
mailchimp, onepassword, retool, segment, sendgrid, snowflake, todoist,
trello, webhook_receiver, zapier) to pick up the remaining three
`*_compliance_e2e.rs` harnesses that still called
`verifier.verify(&req.capability_token, ...)`.

Per the earlier refactor, `fcp-core::CapabilityVerifier::verify` now
takes `T: Into<CapabilityToken>`, and `CapabilityToken` only provides
an owned `From` conversion. Each affected call site is updated the
same way — two per harness (one in `invoke`, one in `plan`):

    -     &req.capability_token,
    +     req.capability_token.clone(),

Cheap clone (a couple of `String`s + a verifying key), keeps `req`
usable for downstream plumbing in the harness. No behavior change
beyond matching the current verifier signature.

Harnesses updated in this commit (3):
- algolia, arxiv, asana
